text.title: Architecture Decision Process Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: ADR lists a single 'chosen' option with no alternatives considered.
antipattern.root-cause: Phase 2 collapsed into Phase 5; options skipped because the architect had a favourite from the start.
antipattern.fix: Reject ADRs that list fewer than 2 considered options; require explicit elimination rationale per discarded option.

antipattern.symptom: Weighted matrix has criteria weights set by the architect alone; stakeholders sign off after the fact.
antipattern.root-cause: Phase 3 weighting treated as a technical input instead of a value statement.
antipattern.fix: Require named stakeholder + signature per criterion weight; reject matrices without it.

antipattern.symptom: Cloud / framework decision approved without a POC; integration risk surfaces during integration sprint.
antipattern.root-cause: Phase 4 skipped to save weeks; one-way reversibility ignored.
antipattern.fix: Branch protection on ADR repo: any ADR with reversibility=one-way requires linked POC artefact.

antipattern.symptom: ADR lands as Accepted with no review date; decision is never re-examined.
antipattern.root-cause: Phase 5 template omitted the review-trigger field.
antipattern.fix: Add review_trigger + review_date to ADR template; calendar reminder created at merge time.
